message:
refactor(stdio): route printf/scanf table imports through OUT_DIR build artefacts

Shift the `include!()` paths for the generated printf and scanf dispatch
tables from the in-tree `stdio/printf_tables.rs` / `stdio/scanf_tables.rs`
snapshots to the cargo build-script output directory:

    include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/stdio_synth/synth/printf_table.rs"))
    include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/stdio_synth/synth/scanf_table.rs"))

The table generator already emits into `$OUT_DIR/stdio_synth/synth/` per
build; the stale in-tree copies were only committed as a bootstrap when
the generator wasn't yet running on every build. Moving the includes to
the OUT_DIR path guarantees that (a) the in-module tables always match
the table-synth pass for the current build, (b) the repo no longer
needs to carry a large generated file that can drift from the
generator, and (c) `cargo clean` + rebuild produces deterministic
tables without relying on whatever snapshot happened to be committed.

No functional change to printf/scanf routing — the PRINTF_TABLE /
SCANF_TABLE re-exports at the module boundary are unchanged; only the
path the `include!` macro reads at compile time moves.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
